description: This figure characterizes the microstructural deformation behavior of Fe–10Al–15Mn–0.8C–5Ni wt% low-density steel under cold rolling (CR) and interrupted tensile deformation at engineering strain e=0.05. It contains 5 subgraphs: (a) EBSD phase map of CR sample, showing co-deformation of deformed γ matrix and deformed B2 intermetallic stringers; (b) parallel EBSD KAM (Kernel Average Misorientation, value range 0-5, proportional to dislocation density) map of CR sample, revealing higher strain localization in softer γ phase than harder B2 phase. (c) TEM bright-field image of A15 sample (annealed at 900℃ for 15 min) after interrupted tensile test, showing morphologies of deformed B2 and γ phases. (d) EBSD phase map of post-tensile A15 sample, identifying B2 precipitates, deformed γ phase, and γ grain boundaries; (e) corresponding KAM map of post-tensile A15 sample, demonstrating geometrically necessary dislocation (GND) localization at γ/B2 interfaces. The results confirm that strain partitioning between γ and B2 phases leads to strain accumulation at phase boundaries, supporting that phase interface dislocation blocking contributes to the measured strain hardening exponent of 0.19-0.27 for the alloy.
